DeSantis expected to make his White House bid official today,"Republicans all but admit they're taking the debt limit hostage. ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ ͏ ‌ By Marita Vlachou Wednesday, May 24 RON DESANTIS EXPECTED TO MAKE HIS PRESIDENTIAL CAMPAIGN OFFICIAL TODAY Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) is expected to announce his long-anticipated White House bid during a livestreamed conversation with Twitter CEO Elon Musk on Wednesday evening. He has long been described as Donald Trump's biggest rival for the 2024 GOP nomination. [HuffPost]HOUSE REPUBLICANS ALL BUT ADMIT THEY'RE TAKING THE DEBT LIMIT 'HOSTAGE' Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) said the silent part out loud by talking about the stalled-but-continuing federal debt limit talks as akin to a hostage negotiation. GOP leadership has also indicated Democrats shouldn't expect to get much, or anything, in return for making concessions. Meanwhile, Republicans, who have insisted on spending cuts, appear to be just fine with government spending — as long as it's on programs they support. [HuffPost]SOUTH CAROLINA LEGISLATURE PASSES 6-WEEK ABORTION BAN South Carolina’s legislature has passed a bill that would ban abortions after six weeks of pregnancy. Under this bill, doctors could face a fine of $10,000 and two years in prison, as well as the loss of their medical license, if they violate the law. [HuffPost]TRUMP'S ATTORNEYS ASK FOR MEETING WITH DOJ AS CLASSIFIED DOCS PROBE NEARS END Trump’s lawyers have requested a meeting with Attorney General Merrick Garland ""to discuss the ongoing injustice that is being perpetrated by your Special Counsel and his prosecutors"" amid reports the probe into the former president's handling of classified documents is nearing an end. [HuffPost] Can you have a healthy amount of narcissism? Experts say yes. How to help your kids learn to speak multiple languages. The funniest marriage tweets to get you through this week. Therapist reveals seven things to avoid when your partner is grieving. Oh good – we've all been eating breakfast the wrong way. Get the latest coronavirus updates on our dedicated page JOHN ROBERTS THINKS SUPREME COURT CAN DO MORE ON ETHICS Chief Justice John Roberts conceded there is more the Supreme Court can do to ""adhere to the highest standards"" of ethical conduct, following reports about the justices’ ethical missteps. But he offered no specifics on reaching that goal. Meanwhile, GOP megadonor Harlan Crow rebuffed a request by Senate Democrats to answer questions pertaining to undisclosed gifts he gave to Justice Clarence Thomas. [AP]MAN ACCUSED OF CRASHING U-HAUL TRUCK NEAR WHITE HOUSE IN PLAN TO 'SEIZE POWER' Authorities said the 19-year-old Missouri man who crashed a U-Haul near the White House began waving around a Nazi flag in the culmination of a six-month plan. He wanted to ""get to the White House, seize power, and be put in charge of the nation,"" and said he would ""kill the president, if that’s what I have to do,"" charges state. [AP]TRUMP HUSH MONEY CASE TRIAL DATE SET Trump’s trial over hush money payments to porn star Stormy Daniels will begin in March 2024, a New York City judge ruled Tuesday. The former president, who has pleaded not guilty to the charges, reportedly scowled upon learning the date. [HuffPost]TARGET REMOVES SOME LGBTQ MERCHANDISE FROM STORES Target is removing certain items from its stores and making other changes to its LGBTQ merchandise nationwide ahead of Pride month, after an intense backlash from some customers including threats toward its workers. [AP]GUAM BRACES FOR TYPHOON MAWAR Typhoon Mawar, a monster storm at Category 4 strength, was set to barrel into Guam on Wednesday as weather officials warned the island should prepare for a “direct hit.” “If you doubt your safety, please get to a shelter immediately,” the National Weather Service said. [HuffPost] What China wants to improve relations with U.S. and why it may be a tall order. Just 11 people filed most school book challenges last year. The castle where future queens drop the royal act. How alcohol really affects your body. Do Americans really want “unbiased” news? Uvalde survivors want safe schools. TikTok has a solution. Panelist slams Greg Gutfeld for praising teacher accused of sex with student. Ex-Trump attorney reveals ""the sad thing"" about possibility of prison sentence. These legendary Black artists left the U.S. — a new generation is following in their footsteps. Comic burns George Santos to his face with a scathing Trump impression. A voice in my head told me my daughter needed to die. Multiple doctors sent me home anyway. GOP donor says Trump should ""drop out"" for two reasons. How to write a good eulogy for a funeral. Julia Fox rocks a completely see-through glass top at Cannes. 14 great under-$30 Father’s Day gifts (and a dad joke for reach one). [opensuse] KDE4 repository change (was: [opensuse-kde] KDE:KDE4 deleted,"Bob S wrote: > On Friday 08 February 2008 04:03:56 am Stephan Binner wrote: >> the old KDE:KDE4 repository is now deleted, for the new structure see: >> http://lists.opensuse.org/opensuse-kde/2008-01/msg00079.html > UUmmmmm.....OK. Structure looks good. Must be just dumb me. How do we go about > adding them? Not so nice the way this was handled. Just giving us the famous ""repodata/repomd.xml not found"" errors. So now server name stays: software.opensuse.org and directory is: /repositories/KDE:/KDE4:/STABLE:/Desktop/openSUSE_10.3/ /repositories/KDE:/KDE4:/STABLE:/Community/openSUSE_10.3/ /repositories/KDE:/KDE4:/STABLE:/Extra-Apps/openSUSE_10.3/ KDE 4.0.1 is still in some ways alpha/beta software though, as central pieces of the user interface are still missing or have to be configured before they can be used by simple users. Kind regards Philippe -- http://lists.opensuse.org/opensuse-kde/2008-01/msg00079.html [opensuse-kde] new repository layout * From: Dirk Mueller * Date: Thu, 31 Jan 2008 17:43:45 +0100 * Message-id: <016344121298.26800.jcfaszqj@xxxxxxx> Hi, given that we have finally decided on a new repository layout, this is how it looks like: KDE:KDE4:STABLE:Desktop - only the bare desktop. it is also the repository to build against in your home project if you want to build your own apps. so it replaces the PLATFORM repository. KDE:KDE4:STABLE:Extra-Apps - addons that are released independently from KDE (amarok, koffice, etc) and maintained by Novell employees (via e.g. openSUSE Factory) KDE:KDE4:STABLE:Community - anything else the same layout exists for UNSTABLE (e.g. KDE 4.1 snapshots): KDE:KDE4:UNSTABLE:Desktop KDE:KDE4:UNSTABLE:Extra-Apps KDE:KDE4:UNSTABLE:Community the 3 repositories are also duplicated as backports, which will contain some of the applications, if they compile against the distro version of Qt, kdelibs and kdebase-runtime. This way you don't have to update your whole KDE just to get a new e.g. amarok or digikam. KDE:KDE4:Backports:Desktop KDE:KDE4:Backports:Extra-Apps KDE:KDE4:Backports:Community The other previously existing KDE4 related repositories under KDE: prefix have already vanished or will disappear in the near future. Greetings, Dirk",0,1

ame was played on November 6, 1869, between Rutgers and Princeton, two college teams. They consisted of 25 players per team and used a round ball that could not be picked up or carried. It could, however, be kicked or batted with the feet, hands, head, or sides, with the objective being to advance it into the opponent's goal. Rutgers won the game 6–4. Collegiate play continued for several years with matches played using the rules of the host school. Representatives of Yale, Columbia, Princeton and Rutgers met on October 19, 1873, to create a standard set of rules for use by all schools. Teams were set at 20 players each, and fields of 400 by 250 feet (122 m × 76 m) were specified. Harvard abstained from the conference, as they favored a rugby-style game that allowed running with the ba",1,1

INVITATION TO THE 79TH IFALPA ANNUAL CONFERENCE,"Dear Andrew, On behalf of the IFALPA local organizing committee and its sponsors, we would like to officially invite you to participate in the 2025 Annual Conference, a key event for IFALPA member associations worldwide. This gathering provides a dedicated platform to strengthen collaboration and unity among pilots, fostering a collective approach to address the challenges facing the global aviation community. This landmark event will be held in Incheon, Republic of Korea from 3rd to 6th of April, 2025.. Special benefits from the local organizing executive committee and its sponsors: The executives committee and its sponsors have negotiated discount rates with major airline operators and hotel management, and they have accepted a 30% discount from our delegates if we are to make group bookings and hotel reservations for them. This is a first come, first-served basis. They sell out fast so book now.. This is first of its kind to support and assist our intended delegates with their trip. Important Information For those who require a visa to South Korea: Due to the new resolutions and recent decisions of the councilors in the immigration department regarding tourist visas to South Korea, due to global issues on terrorism and criminal tendencies of immigrants as this mostly affected those that are coming to South Korea on a tourist visa. This is entirely a new arrangement different from the old system just to check these global vices and also to discourage those without a genuine reason/intention from entering into South Korea. The local organizing executive committee therefore decided to assist the participants with their visa processing for those who require a visa to South Korea. The visa processing is free (no payment is required) and the processing time is 10 working days. We will process your visa here, and then email you a backup documents, and give you a lay-down on who to meet in our Embassy or Consulate in your home country with the command of the South Korean Immigration Bureau to stamp your visa with the document you will receive which will allows you to stay for a maximum of 60 days, it is important that we let you know the process. Delegates who wish to benefit from the 30% discount for the flight booking/hotel reservation and those who need assistance with their visa should email us immediately for further information and guidelines on how to benefit from this gesture. The 79th IFALPA Conference provides a valuable opportunity to stay informed, connect with peers, and contribute to discussions that shape the profession. We look forward to your participation! Warm regards, Capt. Amornvaj (Ben) Mansumitchai President- IFALPA",1,0

Re: Question regarding timeouts.,"Justin Piszcz wrote: > Question-- > > 1. Are these typical to see in the logs? > 2. Some are caused by using fail2ban of course, others by spammers. > > $ grep 'timeout after' mail.log |\\ > sed 's/from/|/g' |\\ > awk '{print ""timeout after"",$8,$10}' |\\ > awk '{print $1,$2,$3}'|sort|uniq -c > 549 timeout after CONNECT > 613 timeout after DATA > 419 timeout after EHLO > 75 timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE > 137 timeout after HELO > 160 timeout after MAIL > 821 timeout after RCPT > 294 timeout after RSET > > 3. However, there are also some legitimate timeouts: > > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after EHLO vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE vger.kernel.org[209.132.176.167] > > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE > qmta01.westchester.pa.mail.comcast.net[76.96.62.16] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE > qmta02.emeryville.ca.mail.comcast.net[76.96.30.24] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE > qmta09.westchester.pa.mail.comcast.net[76.96.62.96] > timeout after END-OF-MESSAGE > are you doing heavy header and body checks? header and body checks should not be abused: these are not a generic content filter. > -- > > For awhile, I have had the following enabled (now using the defaults): > > #smtpd_client_connection_count_limit = 25 > #smtpd_client_connection_rate_limit = 50 > #smtpd_error_sleep_time = 10 > #smtpd_hard_error_limit = 5 > #smtpd_junk_command_limit = 3 > #smtpd_soft_error_limit = 2 > #smtpd_timeout = 60s > > These were mainly extracted from: > http://www.pantz.org/software/postfix/setuppostfix.html > > After replacing these with the defaults: > > 1 200-126-83-1.bk6-dsl.surnet.cl[200.126.83.1] > 1 82.158.130.242.dyn.user.ono.com[82.158.130.242] > 1 84.121.208.253.dyn.user.ono.com[84.121.208.253] > 1 adsl-ull-125-230.49-151.net24.it[151.49.230.125] > 1 gwfry2-1-190.802.cz[85.132.248.190] > 1 pool-72-77-86-211.pitbpa.fios.verizon.net[72.77.86.211] > 2 172-143-89-200.fibertel.com.ar[200.89.143.172] > 2 h222.144.16.98.ip.alltel.net[98.16.144.222] > 3 fl-71-52-8-35.dhcp.embarqhsd.net[71.52.8.35] > 4 ppp340D.dsl.pacific.net.au[203.143.213.13] > 9 88-199-92-2.tktelekom.pl[88.199.92.2] > 9 n24z185l52.broadband.ctm.net[202.86.185.52] > > I still get various timeout after (X) but as you can tell, they are > most likely all spam/zombie hosts (most likely blocked via > iptables/fail2ban). No, they probably disconnected themselves. I often see many of these, and in different networks with different OSes and different configurations (without fail2ban or equivalent), so the reason is at the zombie end, not at the MX end. In the CONNECT case, most likely the reverse DNS lookup took longer than the zombie could wait (maybe zombie authoris prefer servers that don't do any checks, including rDNS lookup, as this means better deliverability for their junk?). Other possibilities include the zombie crash/reboot/disconnected/... etc. > > # postconf -n > address_verify_map = btree:/var/spool/postfix/etc/verify > alias_database = hash:/etc/aliases > alias_maps = hash:/etc/aliases > allow_percent_hack = no > anvil_rate_time_unit = 60s > anvil_status_update_time = 1d > append_dot_mydomain = yes > biff = no > body_checks = pcre:/etc/postfix/body_checks.pcre > broken_sasl_auth_clients = yes > config_directory = /etc/postfix > daemon_timeout = 2d > delay_warning_time = 4h > disable_vrfy_command = yes > header_checks = pcre:/etc/postfix/header_checks.pcre > html_directory = /usr/share/doc/postfix/html > inet_interfaces = all > mailbox_command = procmail -a ""$EXTENSION"" > mailbox_size_limit = 0 > message_size_limit = 0 > mime_header_checks = $header_checks > mydestination = $myhostname, localhost.$mydomain, localhost, $mydomain > mydomain = localdomain > myhostname = > mynetworks = 127.0.0.0/8 192.168.0.0/24 > myorigin = $myhostname > nested_header_checks = $header_checks > readme_directory = /usr/share/doc/postfix > recipient_delimiter = + > relayhost = show_user_unknown_table_name = no > smtp_bind_address = > smtpd_banner = $myhostname remove this. the default is good. > smtpd_client_restrictions = smtpd_data_restrictions = > reject_unauth_pipelining ahuh? this should be smtpd_client_restrictions= smtpd_data_restrictions = reject_unauth_pipelining you have a leading whitespace before your smtpd_data_restrictions. remove it. > smtpd_delay_reject = yes > smtpd_discard_ehlo_keyword_address_maps = > cidr:/etc/postfix/ehlo_keyword_map.cidr > smtpd_end_of_data_restrictions = reject_unauth_pipelining > smtpd_etrn_restrictions = permit_mynetworks, reject > smtpd_helo_required = yes > smtpd_helo_restrictions = smtpd_policy_service_max_idle = 600s > smtpd_policy_service_timeout = 600s > smtpd_recipient_restrictions = permitlocal, relaycheck, > presenderchecks, prependheader, prewhitelists, > stndchecks, whitelists, pcrechecks, > cidrchecks, hashchecks, unlistchecks, > spfcheck, rhsblchecks, policydcheck, > greylist > smtpd_restriction_classes = cidrchecks greylist > hashchecks pcrechecks permitlocal > policydcheck prependheader presenderchecks > prewhitelists rblchecks relaycheck > rhsblchecks spamchecks spfcheck > stndchecks testblists unlistchecks > verifysender whitelists unofrtunately, postconf -n does not show restrictions classes (what checks they contain), so we have no idea what checks you are actually doing. > smtpd_sasl_auth_enable = yes > smtpd_sasl_local_domain = $mydomain > smtpd_sasl_security_options = noanonymous > smtpd_sender_restrictions = soft_bounce = no > strict_rfc821_envelopes = yes > swap_bangpath = no > unverified_sender_reject_code = 550 > > Justin.",0,1

I would greatly appreciate any advice or support you can provide to help us contain and resolve this situation at the earliest possible time. Please let me know the necessary steps to be taken. Thank you for promptly attending to this issue.
